<!-- Auto-generated cross-topic map — 2026-04-15 -->
# Cross-Topic Relationships

constraint-theory ↔ fragile-constraints: Constraint strength spectrum and failure modes; fragile-constraints analyzes when/how constraint-theory breaks down

constraint-theory ↔ interface-shapes-cognition: Interfaces instantiate constraints; constraint prescription determines what cognitive paths are available to agents

agent-architecture ↔ middleware-as-organ: Middleware is core infrastructure enabling mini-agent's distributed execution and multi-lane concurrency patterns

agent-architecture ↔ agent-tools: Tools are the external execution surface; architecture determines tool dispatch logic and convergence semantics

memory-architecture ↔ feedback_dual_memory_root: Dual-memory-root (instance vs workspace directories) is the specific pattern solving memory-architecture's identity/isolation boundary

brocards-as-convergence-conditions ↔ constraint-theory: Brocards operationalize abstract constraint theory at the identity level; convergence conditions make constraints testable

teaching-monster ↔ prediction-calibration: Arena rankings require calibrated confidence scores; dogfooding TM revealed calibration gaps in judgment heuristics

knowledge-tensions ↔ isc-concept-index: ISC-index organizes concepts; knowledge-tensions maps contradictions between source positions on those same concepts

cross-level-constraint-factorization ↔ interface-shapes-cognition: Both analyze constraint distribution across levels (architectural/cognitive/policy); interfaces are the factorization mechanism

scaffolding-theory ↔ agent-architecture: Scaffolding principles (progressive complexity, reversible operations) inform mini-agent's capability layering and delegation primitives
